info.title: MM-V2 Trading API
info.description:
## High-Frequency Market Making Engine API

This API powers the MM-V2 market making system, providing:

### For Trading Clients
- **Orders**: View and track orders placed on exchanges (Polymarket, Kalshi, etc.)
- **Bets**: View hedge bets placed on bookmakers
- **Fixtures**: Access event/fixture metadata and scores
- **Positions**: Get aggregated exposure across markets

### For Operations
- **Emergency Controls**: Cancel all orders, pause/resume trading
- **Monitoring**: System status, connection health, latency metrics
- **Real-time Updates**: WebSocket subscriptions for live data

### Authentication
Client endpoints require the `X-API-Key` header with a valid UUID.
Admin endpoints are typically protected at the network level.

### Rate Limits
- REST API: 2,000 requests/minute per client
- WebSocket: 5 connections per API key

### Quick Start
1. Click **Authorize** button above
2. Enter your API key (UUID format)
3. All client endpoints will use this key automatically

get.description: Returns the authenticated client's information based on their API key.

patch.description:
Update authenticated client preferences.

    Currently supports persisting the preferred odds display format used by the UI.

get.description:
Returns the authenticated client's trading status.

    This includes:
    - Whether the client is under manual pause
    - Whether global soft pause or emergency is blocking trading
    - Whether trading is currently active for this client

post.description:
Manually pause market making for the authenticated client.

    Behavior:
    - Existing resting exchange orders are left on the exchange
    - No new orders are posted for this client
    - Fill hedging still runs
    - Hedge-triggered reposts are blocked while paused

Resume market making for the authenticated client after a manual pause.

    Resume clears only the client's manual pause flag. Global soft pause or
    emergency mode can still block trading if they are active.

Fully stop market making for the authenticated client.

    Behavior:
    - Cancel all currently open exchange orders for this client
    - Block new order placement and reposts until `/trading/start`
    - Fill hedging can still run for any in-flight exchange fills that arrive later

Start market making for the authenticated client after a manual pause or stop.

    This clears client-scoped manual controls only. Global soft pause or system
    stop/emergency can still block trading if they are active.

get.description: Returns sports currently in the oddspapi feed for your bookmaker, with live fixture counts.

schema.title: Response List Sports Api V1 Sports Get

get.description: Returns tournaments currently in the oddspapi feed, grouped by category, with activation status.

schema.title: Response List Tournaments For Sport Api V1 Sports  Sport Id  Tournaments Get

get.summary: List hedgeable market types for a sport with per-client toggle status
get.description: Discovery endpoint backing the markets-page Market types tab. Returns each `(marketType, playerProp)` group that is hedgeable on at least one of the client's configured trading exchanges, joined against the per-client allowlist deny set so the caller can render `enabled` toggles. Hedgeability is empirical: a market type appears if the `odds` table has at least one mapping row for an outcome of that type on one of the client's exchanges (mm-v2-1os). Non-hedgeable types are not returned at all.

schema.title: Response List Market Types For Sport Api V1 Sports  Sport Id  Market Types Get

patch.summary: Bulk-toggle market types for the authenticated client
patch.description: Upserts allowlist rows for each `(marketType, playerProp)` in `updates`. For any row set to `enabled=false`, immediately cancels open orders owned by this client whose `outcomeId` belongs to that market type for the given sport. Bookmaker is resolved server-side.

schema.title: Response Patch Market Allowlist Api V1 Client Market Allowlist Patch

post.summary: Restore default-on for every market type in this scope
post.description: Deletes all `client_market_allowlist` rows for `(client, bookmaker, sportId)`. Under the missing-row-equals-enabled contract, this restores every market type to default-on. No order cancellation — we are enabling, not disabling.

schema.title: Response Activate All Market Types Api V1 Client Market Allowlist Activate All Post

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of orders for your client account.

    Orders are sorted by creation date (newest first) and can be filtered by:
    - **fixtureId**: Filter by specific fixture/event
    - **exchange**: Filter by exchange (polymarket, kalshi, etc.)
    - **status**: Filter by order status (PLACED, FILLED, CANCELLED, etc.)

    Use pagination parameters to navigate through large result sets.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of hedge bets placed on your behalf.

    Bets are created when orders are matched on exchanges. They represent
    the hedging activity on the bookmaker side.

    Filter by:
    - **orderId**: Get bets for a specific order
    - **bookmaker**: Filter by bookmaker (vertex, pinnacle, demo)
    - **status**: Filter by bet status - supports comma-separated values (e.g., `placed,pending`)
      - `placed`: Successfully placed bets
      - `pending`: Bets awaiting confirmation
      - `declined`: Rejected bets

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of fixtures (events/matches).

    Fixtures contain event metadata like participants, start time, and scores.

    - **tournamentId**: Filter by tournament (e.g., 234 for NHL)
    - **sportId**: Filter by sport (e.g., 1 for Soccer)
    - **live**: Filter by live status (true/false)
    - **all**: Set to true to see all available fixtures (not just ones with your orders)

get.description:
Get available markets for a sport from cached odds data.

    **Note:** Markets are global reference data - they describe what market types
    exist (1x2, Over/Under, etc.) and are not specific to any client. All clients
    see the same market definitions.

    Markets are aggregated from all active fixtures for the specified sport.

    **Required:**
    - **sportId**: Get markets for a sport (e.g., 1=Soccer, 14=Ice Hockey)

    **Response fields:**
    - `marketId`: Market identifier
    - `outcomeIds`: List of outcome IDs available for this market
    - `playerIds`: List of player IDs (for player prop markets)
    - `bookmakers`: Bookmakers offering this market

parameters.name: sportId

schema.description: Sport ID (required)
schema.title: Sportid

parameters.description: Sport ID (required)

schema.title: Response List Markets Api V1 Markets Get

get.description:
Get players for a sport from cached odds data.

    **Note:** Players are global reference data - they describe what players
    have prop markets available. All clients see the same player data.

    Players are aggregated from all active fixtures for the specified sport
    where playerId > 0 (player prop markets).

    **Required:**
    - **sportId**: Get players for a sport (e.g., 1=Soccer, 14=Ice Hockey)

    **Response fields:**
    - `playerId`: Player identifier
    - `marketIds`: Markets this player has props in
    - `outcomeIds`: Outcome IDs for this player
    - `bookmakers`: Bookmakers offering props for this player

parameters.name: sportId

schema.description: Sport ID (required)
schema.title: Sportid

parameters.description: Sport ID (required)

schema.title: Response List Players Api V1 Players Get

get.description:
Get aggregated positions for your client account.

    A position aggregates all orders for a specific fixture/outcome/exchange combination,
    showing total stake, matched stake, and open stake.

    This gives you a consolidated view of your exposure on each market.

    **Status filters** (supports comma-separated values, e.g., `open,matched`):
    - `open`: Only positions with open orders (still on exchange, not fully matched)
    - `matched`: Only positions with matched stake > 0 (excludes cancelled)
    - `filled`: Only fully matched orders
    - `cancelled`: Only cancelled orders
    - `active`: Exclude cancelled orders (default)

    Examples:
    - `/positions?status=open` - Only open positions
    - `/positions?status=open,matched` - Open or matched positions
    - `/positions?status=filled` - Only filled positions

    Optionally filter by date range to see positions from a specific time period.

parameters.name: fixtureId

schema.description: Filter by fixture ID
schema.title: Fixtureid

parameters.description: Filter by fixture ID

parameters.name: exchange

schema.description: Filter by exchange
schema.title: Exchange

parameters.description: Filter by exchange

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status - comma-separated (e.g., 'open,matched'). Options: open, matched, filled, cancelled, active
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status - comma-separated (e.g., 'open,matched'). Options: open, matched, filled, cancelled, active

get.description:
Market-making P&L statistics for your client account.

    The engine quotes on exchanges (Polymarket, Kalshi, ...) and hedges every
    fill on a bookmaker. P&L is reported in terms of the spread captured across
    the two legs, not as directional bet P&L.

    **totalTurnover** — Σ stake successfully laid off on the bookmaker leg.
    This is the volume the engine actually closed, not the quoted exchange stake.

    **validTurnover** — Risk-adjusted turnover on the bookmaker leg.
    Computed as Σ ABS(bet P&L) per settled bet. Accounts for odds: a $100
    bet at 1.1 contributes less valid turnover than $100 at 3.0.

    **netRealizedSpread** — Σ (exchange_leg_pnl + bookmaker_leg_pnl) across
    pairs where both legs have reached a terminal state and at least one hedge
    was placed successfully. Absolute dollars.

    **nakedPnl** — Directional P&L from settled exchange fills that never
    got a successful hedge. Tracked separately so it does not pollute the
    spread number.

    **unhedgedStake** — Absolute $ of matched exchange stake that never
    reached a successful hedge.

    **unhedgedStakePct** — % of matched exchange stake that never reached a
    successful hedge (quantity slippage on the hedge leg).

    **avgHedgePriceDrift** — Stake-weighted mean hedgeOddsDrift on successful
    hedges, in decimal-odds points (price slippage on the hedge leg).

    Date filter applies to order createdAt.

parameters.name: fromDate

schema.description: Filter orders created on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
schema.title: Fromdate

parameters.description: Filter orders created on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: toDate

schema.description: Filter orders created on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
schema.title: Todate

parameters.description: Filter orders created on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

get.description: Basic health check for container orchestration (Kubernetes liveness probe).
Readiness check for container orchestration (Kubernetes readiness probe). Verifies all dependencies are available.
Prometheus-format metrics endpoint for monitoring.

## Real-time Data Subscription (WebSocket)

**URL:** `ws://localhost:8081/ws/subscribe`

**Connection Limits:** Maximum 5 concurrent connections per API key.

### Protocol

1. **Connect** to `/ws/subscribe`

2. **Subscribe** (within 30 seconds):
```json
{
  "type": "subscribe",
  "api_key": "your-client-api-key-uuid",
  "channels": ["orders", "bets", "accounts", "scores", "emergency"]
}
```

3. **Confirmation**:
```json
{"type": "subscribed", "subscription_id": 1, "channels": ["orders", "bets"]}
```

4. **Receive broadcasts**:
```json
{
  "type": "broadcast",
  "channel": "orders",
  "event": "UPDATE",
  "payload": {"id": 123, "orderStatus": "FILLED"},
  "old": {"orderStatus": "PLACED"}
}
```

5. **Keep-alive**: Send `{"type": "ping"}` → Receive `{"type": "pong"}`

6. **Heartbeat**: Server sends `{"type": "heartbeat"}` every 60s

7. **Unsubscribe**: Send `{"type": "unsubscribe"}`

### Channels

| Channel | Description | Filtered |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| `orders` | Order placement, updates, fills | By client |
| `bets` | Hedge bet updates | By client |
| `accounts` | Balance changes | By client |
| `scores` | Live score updates | Global |
| `emergency` | Emergency mode status | Global |

### Error Codes

- `api_key required` - Missing api_key
- `Invalid api_key format` - Must be UUID
- `Invalid API key` - Not found or inactive
- `Connection limit exceeded` - Max 5 per API key

example.detail: This endpoint requires a WebSocket connection. Use ws://host:port/ws/subscribe

get.description: Get statistics about active WebSocket subscriptions.

schema.title: Response Websocket Subscribe Stats Ws Subscribe Stats Get

post.description:
**⚠️ ADMIN ONLY - AFFECTS ALL CLIENTS**

        **CAUTION: This will cancel ALL open orders for ALL clients across ALL exchanges.**

        Trigger system-wide emergency mode from an external source (monitoring system, dashboard, etc.).
        This is useful for:
        - Dashboard "kill switch" button
        - External monitoring detecting issues
        - Manual intervention from operations team

        When triggered:
        1. All new order placement is stopped FOR ALL CLIENTS
        2. All open orders are cancelled on all exchanges FOR ALL CLIENTS
        3. System enters emergency mode

        To recover, use `/emergency/resume` after resolving the issue.

        **For client-specific cancellation, use `/api/v1/orders/cancel-all` instead.**

parameters.name: X-Admin-Key

schema.description: Admin API key for emergency operations
schema.title: X-Admin-Key

parameters.description: Admin API key for emergency operations

post.description:
**⚠️ ADMIN ONLY - AFFECTS ALL CLIENTS**

        Canonical system-wide stop endpoint.

        This will:
        1. Stop new order placement for all clients
        2. Cancel all open orders on all exchanges
        3. Keep the system stopped until `/emergency/start`

parameters.name: X-Admin-Key

schema.description: Admin API key for emergency operations
schema.title: X-Admin-Key

parameters.description: Admin API key for emergency operations

post.description:
**⚠️ ADMIN ONLY - AFFECTS ALL CLIENTS**

        Deprecated alias for `/emergency/stop`.

        This performs a full manual system stop:
        - blocks new order placement for all clients
        - cancels all open orders on all exchanges
        - requires manual resume/start

post.description:
**⚠️ ADMIN ONLY**

        Canonical system-wide start endpoint.

        This clears manual system stop/emergency mode, reloads snapshots, and
        allows normal order processing to resume.

post.description:
**⚠️ ADMIN ONLY**

        Resume trading after emergency/pause.

        This will:
        1. Clear emergency mode
        2. Reload initial snapshots from OddsPAPI REST API
        3. Resume normal order processing

        Only call this after the underlying issue has been resolved.

tags.name: Client API
tags.description: Endpoints for trading clients to access orders, bets, fixtures, and positions. Requires `X-API-Key` header authentication.

tags.name: Emergency Controls
tags.description: **ADMIN ONLY** - System-wide emergency controls affecting ALL clients. For client-specific cancellation, use `/api/v1/orders/cancel-all`.

tags.name: Order Slots
tags.description: **ADMIN ONLY** - Inspect and manage order slot states, poison recovery, and reconciliation.

tags.name: Status & Monitoring
tags.description: System status, health checks, latency metrics, and connection status.

tags.name: WebSocket
tags.description: Real-time data subscriptions for orders, bets, accounts, and scores.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description: View current system configuration (non-sensitive values only).
